refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is solution-neutral as it does not specify how to achieve this, only what is required. Therefore, it is atomic and does not need further decomposition.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Incorporate error detection and correction mechanisms to recover lost packets and ensure communication quality
Design redundant communication paths to provide failover and reduce effective packet loss
Establish real-time communication quality monitoring with feedback control to dynamically optimize network usage
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and protocol stack implementations
Interaction with error correction mechanisms if implemented separately
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical approach to maintain packet loss limits by adapting communication parameters, which can be modularized independently from other system functions. This supports functional independence and clear mapping from FR to DP.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be monitored accurately and parameters adjusted in real-time
Risk that adaptation may not fully compensate for sudden network degradation leading to packet loss exceeding limits
Assumes compliance with worksite safety and operational policies does not restrict communication protocol adjustments
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including error detection and correction as complementary DPs or as part of the adaptive protocol to enhance robustness against packet loss.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network gateway and communication management components responsible for handling communication protocols and network parameter adjustments.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network status data and send communication performance metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network connection for adjusting transmission parameters and monitoring packet loss.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol logically belongs to network management; minimal diffusion as it interfaces mainly with network and telemetry.
Lower cohesion since vehicle module focuses on vehicle control and status; adaptive communication would require significant cross-module interaction.
Moderate cohesion as teleoperation depends on communication but does not implement protocol adaptation itself; moderate diffusion expected.
Low cohesion; safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not communication protocol management; high diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the adaptive communication protocol because it directly manages network communication parameters and can contain the logic with minimal diffusion outside the module.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may cause some coupling.
If network link interface changes, it may require updates in other modules interacting with NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying an adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within customer-defined limits, fulfilling the requirement for teleoperation communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may cause some coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk, as it logically contains the adaptive communication protocol and interfaces mainly with network and telemetry. Other modules have lower containment scores and higher diffusion risks, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and ensuring packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution module is clear, as it encompasses the network gateway and communication management components responsible for the required functionality.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document the dependency on telemetry data and define interface contracts to minimize coupling and facilitate future changes.
Monitor and manage potential impacts of changes in the Network Link interface on other interacting modules to reduce maintenance risks.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and the containment of the implementation module is strong with manageable diffusion risks. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary decomposition that could complicate verification and increase risk. Recommended revisions focus on documentation and interface management rather than further decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly document the dependency on telemetry data and define interface contracts to minimize coupling and facilitate future changes, as well as to monitor and manage potential impacts of changes in the Network Link interface on other modules to reduce maintenance risks.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol sufficiently meets the teleoperation communication quality requirements.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary for this FR and DP.
open_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules causing coupling.
Possible impacts of changes in the Network Link interface on interacting modules.
evidence_needed: Verification results from monitoring packet loss metrics under agreed network conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and dependency management strategies.
